infoHub.title: Reimbursement Systems Guide
infoHub.description: Learn about reimbursement codes and application processes for major markets

US.name: United States
US.description: The US healthcare reimbursement system is a complex multi-payer environment including Medicare (federal program for 65+), Medicaid (state programs for low-income), and private commercial insurers. Medical devices require specific billing codes (CPT, HCPCS) and often need coverage determinations before payers will reimburse.

CPT.name: Current Procedural Terminology
CPT.description: AMA-maintained codes for physician services and procedures. Category I codes are established procedures, Category II for performance measures, Category III for emerging technology (temporary codes valid for 5 years). Medical device companies typically pursue Category III codes first for new technologies.

HCPCS_Level_II.name: Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System
HCPCS_Level_II.description: CMS-managed alphanumeric codes (A0000-V9999) for supplies, equipment, DME, prosthetics, and non-physician services. Key categories: A-codes (ambulance/transport), E-codes (DME), L-codes (orthotics/prosthetics), K-codes (temporary). Critical for device-specific reimbursement separate from procedure codes.

MS_DRG.name: Medicare Severity-Diagnosis Related Groups
MS_DRG.description: Prospective payment groupings for hospital inpatient stays. Hospitals receive a fixed payment per DRG regardless of actual costs. New technologies may qualify for add-on payments (NTAP) if they demonstrate substantial clinical improvement.

APC.name: Ambulatory Payment Classifications
APC.description: Outpatient prospective payment system groupings. Similar to DRGs but for hospital outpatient services. Pass-through payments available for new devices meeting cost thresholds.

ICD_10.name: International Classification of Diseases
ICD_10.description: Diagnosis codes (ICD-10-CM) and procedure codes (ICD-10-PCS) used to justify medical necessity and document procedures performed. Essential for claims processing and coverage determinations.

US.governingBody: American Medical Association (CPT), Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (HCPCS/DRG/APC), State Medicaid Agencies
US.applicationProcess: CPT: Submit Category III code application to AMA CPT Editorial Panel (meets 3x/year). HCPCS: Submit coding request to CMS via HCPCS workgroup (quarterly meetings). Coverage: Submit NCD/LCD request to CMS or MAC. NTAP: Apply to CMS for new technology add-on payment during IPPS rulemaking cycle.
US.typicalTimeline: CPT Category III: 6-12 months; HCPCS: 6-12 months; NCD: 9-12 months; NTAP: 12-18 months (tied to fiscal year rulemaking)

EU.name: European Union
EU.description: Country-specific systems; major markets include Germany (EBM/G-DRG), France (CCAM/GHS)

VARIES.name: National Reimbursement Codes
VARIES.description: Varies by EU member state - refer to national HTA agencies

EU.governingBody: National HTA Bodies (e.g., G-BA in Germany, HAS in France)
EU.applicationProcess: Varies by country - typically requires health economic dossier
EU.typicalTimeline: 12-36 months depending on country and device classification

UK.name: United Kingdom
UK.description: NHS with OPCS-4 procedure codes and HRG tariff groupings

OPCS_4.name: Office of Population Censuses and Surveys Classification v4

UK.governingBody: NHS England, NICE (National Institute for Health and Care Excellence)

JP.name: Japan
JP.description: Universal Health Insurance with procedure and material reimbursement codes
JP.governingBody: MHLW (Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare)
JP.applicationProcess: Shonin Shinsei (approval application) followed by reimbursement application
JP.typicalTimeline: 18-36 months including PMDA review and pricing

CA.name: Canada
CA.description: Provincial systems with CCI for procedures and CMG for acute inpatient

CMG.name: Case Mix Groups
CMG.description: Patient grouping methodology for acute inpatient

CA.governingBody: CIHI (Canadian Institute for Health Information), Provincial Ministries
CA.applicationProcess: Provincial submissions; CADTH for HTA review

AU.name: Australia
AU.description: Medicare Benefits Schedule (MBS) for medical services and prostheses list
AU.governingBody: Department of Health, MSAC (Medical Services Advisory Committee)
AU.applicationProcess: MSAC application for new MBS items; Prostheses List Application

overview.title: EU Reimbursement Landscape
overview.description: Unlike the centralized US system, the European Union does not have a single unified reimbursement framework. Each EU member state operates its own national healthcare system with distinct reimbursement codes, HTA processes, and pricing mechanisms. Understanding country-specific requirements is essential for successful EU market entry.

keyPrinciples.nationalSovereignty: National sovereignty: Each EU country controls its own healthcare budget and reimbursement decisions
keyPrinciples.htaVariation: HTA variation: Health Technology Assessment requirements and timelines differ significantly by country
keyPrinciples.pricingDiversity: Pricing diversity: Device pricing and reimbursement rates are negotiated at the national or regional level
keyPrinciples.ceMarkingNotReimbursement: CE marking is NOT reimbursement: EU regulatory approval (CE Mark) does not guarantee reimbursement
keyPrinciples.regionalDifferences: Regional differences: Some countries (Germany, Spain, Italy) have additional regional/federal variations

tooltips.datePicker: Select the time period for your commercial performance analysis. Use date ranges to compare year-over-year trends or focus on specific months. The system will filter all data and forecasts based on your selection.
tooltips.uploadCSV: Upload financial data via CSV file. Ensure your file includes columns for Product Name, Month (YYYY-MM format), Revenue, and Costs. Optional columns: Market, Currency, Notes. The system will validate data and provide import feedback.
tooltips.smartForecasting: AI-powered revenue forecasting that incorporates product relationships, market intelligence, and seasonal factors. The system generates multiple scenarios (conservative, expected, optimistic) with confidence levels. Higher confidence indicates more reliable predictions based on data quality and market stability.
tooltips.productRelationships: Configure how your products work together to drive revenue. Set up main products (primary devices) and their relationships to accessories, consumables, or services. Define multipliers to model how selling one product impacts sales of related products over time.
tooltips.initialMultiplier: One-time revenue boost when a main product is sold. For example, 1.5x means the relationship generates 50% additional revenue initially. Use conservative estimates (1.1-1.3x) for new markets, typical estimates (1.3-2.0x) for established relationships, or aggressive estimates (2.0x+) for strong platform effects.
tooltips.recurringMultiplier: Ongoing revenue impact from recurring purchases like consumables or services. For example, 0.3x monthly means the accessory generates 30% of the main product's value each month. Consider customer usage patterns, consumable lifecycles, and competitive factors.
tooltips.lifecycleDuration: How long the product relationship remains active (in months). Consider factors like customer switching costs, technology lifecycle, competitive pressure, and contract terms. Typical ranges: consumables (12-24 months), services (24-60 months), platforms (60+ months).
tooltips.seasonalityFactors: Monthly adjustment factors to account for seasonal demand variations. Values above 1.0 indicate higher demand months, below 1.0 indicate lower demand. Consider healthcare budget cycles, seasonal procedures, holiday impacts, and regional variations in your market.
tooltips.aiPrognosisFactors: Market intelligence factors that influence your revenue forecasts. The AI analyzes market dynamics (25% weight), technology factors (20%), economic indicators (20%), customer behavior (20%), and company-specific factors (15%). Review these insights to understand forecast drivers and market opportunities.
tooltips.confidenceLevels: Forecast reliability indicator. High confidence (85-95%): strong historical data and stable market conditions. Medium confidence (65-85%): moderate data with some market uncertainty. Low confidence (<65%): limited data or volatile market conditions. Use confidence levels to guide decision-making and risk assessment.
tooltips.rnpvIntegration: Integrate commercial performance forecasts with risk-adjusted Net Present Value analysis. The system automatically feeds revenue projections into financial valuation models, considering regulatory risk, market risk, and development timelines for comprehensive investment analysis.
tooltips.financialDataTable: View and manage all financial data including revenue, costs, and profitability metrics by product and time period. Use filters to focus on specific products or markets. Click columns to sort data. Export data for external analysis or reporting.

smartCostOverview.title: Smart Cost Intelligence Overview
smartCostOverview.description: AI-powered cost estimation and market analysis
smartCostOverview.content:
# Smart Cost Intelligence System

The Smart Cost Intelligence system provides AI-powered cost estimation for medical device market extensions, combining regulatory data, device complexity, and market intelligence.

## Key Features

### 1. Market-Specific Cost Templates
- Validated cost data for major global markets
- Regulatory submission fees and requirements
- Clinical trial costs and timelines
- Marketing and distribution expenses

### 2. Device Complexity Intelligence
- Automatic cost adjustments based on device class
- Risk-based multipliers for accuracy
- Specialized handling for IVD and SaMD devices
- Regulatory pathway optimization

### 3. Scenario Planning
- Conservative: Safe, higher-end cost estimates (+15-25%)
- Typical: Balanced, market-standard costs (baseline)
- Aggressive: Optimistic, lower-end estimates (-10-20%)

### 4. Smart Adjustments
- Real-time currency conversion
- Inflation modeling for multi-year timelines
- Market-specific economic factors
- Historical cost validation

## How to Use

1. Select Market: Choose your target market extension
2. Configure Device Class: System auto-detects complexity
3. Choose Scenario: Pick conservative, typical, or aggressive
4. Review Templates: Examine market-specific cost categories
5. Apply Smart Estimates: Use AI-calculated costs or customize
6. Analyze Adjustments: Review detailed cost breakdowns

## Benefits

- Accuracy: Market-validated cost data
- Speed: Instant cost estimation
- Intelligence: AI-powered adjustments
- Transparency: Detailed breakdown of all factors
- Flexibility: Easy customization and scenario testing

deviceClassMultipliers.title: Understanding Device Class Cost Multipliers
deviceClassMultipliers.description: How device complexity affects regulatory costs
deviceClassMultipliers.content:
# Device Class Cost Multipliers

The system automatically adjusts costs based on your device's regulatory classification and complexity.

## Classification Impact

### Class I Devices
- Base Multiplier: 1.0x
- Regulatory: 1.0x (minimal requirements)
- Clinical: 0.8x (often exempt)
- Example: Simple bandages, basic surgical instruments

### Class II Devices
- Base Multiplier: 1.2x
- Regulatory: 1.5x (510(k) required)
- Clinical: 1.2x (often required)
- Example: Infusion pumps, surgical lasers

### Class III Devices
- Base Multiplier: 1.8x
- Regulatory: 2.5x (PMA required)
- Clinical: 2.0x (extensive studies)
- Example: Heart valves, implantable defibrillators

### IVD Devices
- Base Multiplier: Variable (1.1-2.0x)
- Risk-based: A (1.1x) to D (2.0x)
- Example: Blood glucose meters, COVID tests

### SaMD (Software as Medical Device)
- Base Multiplier: Variable (1.0-1.8x)
- Risk categories: I (1.0x) to IV (1.8x)
- Example: Diagnostic imaging software, clinical decision support

## Regional Variations

Different markets apply these multipliers differently:
- EU MDR: Classification rules
- Canada: Health Canada classes
- Japan: PMDA classification

## Smart Adjustments

The system considers:
- Predicate device availability
- Novel technology factors
- Combination device complexity
- Software integration requirements

costScenarioPlanning.title: Cost Scenario Planning & Risk Management
costScenarioPlanning.description: Strategic cost modeling for different business scenarios
costScenarioPlanning.content:
# Cost Scenario Planning

Strategic cost modeling helps you plan for different business and regulatory scenarios.

## Scenario Types

### Conservative Scenario (+15-25%)
When to Use:
- First-time market entry
- Novel or complex devices
- Uncertain regulatory pathway
- Risk-averse planning

Characteristics:
- Higher regulatory costs
- Extended timelines
- Additional clinical requirements
- Safety margins for unknowns

### Typical Scenario (Baseline)
When to Use:
- Standard market extensions
- Well-understood devices
- Clear regulatory pathway
- Balanced risk/opportunity

Characteristics:
- Market-standard costs
- Normal timelines
- Expected requirements
- Validated assumptions

### Aggressive Scenario (-10-20%)
When to Use:
- Fast-follower strategy
- Streamlined devices
- Favorable regulatory environment
- Optimistic planning

Characteristics:
- Optimized costs
- Accelerated timelines
- Minimal additional requirements
- Best-case assumptions

## Best Practices

1. Always model multiple scenarios
2. Document assumptions clearly
3. Update as new information emerges
4. Consider scenario probability weighting
5. Align scenarios with business strategy

currencyInflation.title: Currency Conversion & Inflation Modeling
currencyInflation.description: Financial modeling for multi-year, multi-market projects
currencyInflation.content:
# Currency & Inflation Intelligence

Advanced financial modeling for accurate multi-year, multi-market cost projections.

## Currency Conversion

### Real-Time Exchange Rates
- Live market rates updated daily
- Historical rate analysis
- Volatility considerations
- Regional cost differentials

### Market-Specific Costs
- Local regulatory fees (not just converted)
- Regional service provider rates
- Market-specific requirements
- Local economic factors

## Inflation Modeling

### Automatic Inflation Adjustments
- Configurable annual inflation rates
- Compound inflation calculations
- Years-to-launch consideration
- Regional economic variations

### Smart Inflation Rates
- Conservative: 4-6% annually
- Typical: 2-4% annually (default 3%)
- Aggressive: 1-3% annually

## Best Practices

### Currency Management
1. Use local currency data when available
2. Monitor exchange rate trends
3. Consider currency hedging strategies
4. Plan for volatility scenarios

### Inflation Planning
1. Use conservative estimates for long timelines
2. Adjust rates for different cost categories
3. Update projections regularly
4. Consider economic cycle timing

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started with XYREG
gettingStarted.description: Complete guide to getting started with the platform
gettingStarted.content:
# Getting Started with XYREG

Welcome to the XYREG Medical Device Regulatory Management Platform! This comprehensive platform helps you manage medical device compliance across multiple regulatory frameworks.

## First Steps
1. Complete your profile - Add your company information and role
2. Set up your first company - Configure phases, documents, and templates
3. Create your first product - Start managing your device lifecycle
4. Upload documents - Begin building your regulatory documentation
5. Configure compliance frameworks - Set up EU MDR, FDA, or other regulatory requirements

## Key Features Overview
- Mission Control: Multi-company dashboard with role switching
- Company Dashboard: Complete product portfolio management
- Product Management: Track devices through regulatory phases
- Document Control: Comprehensive document management system
- Gap Analysis: Automated compliance assessments
- Audit Management: Track findings and corrective actions
- Classification Tools: SaMD, IVDR, EU MDR classification wizards
- Business Analysis: NPV calculations and commercial planning

## Platform Navigation
- Use the sidebar to navigate between features
- Switch between companies using the company dropdown
- Access contextual help using the help button (F1 key)
- Use the Mission Control for multi-company overview

missionControl.title: Mission Control Dashboard
missionControl.content:
# Mission Control Dashboard

Mission Control provides a comprehensive overview of all companies you have access to, enabling efficient multi-company management.

## Dashboard Overview
- Company Grid: Visual overview of all your companies
- Role Indicators: See your role in each company (Admin, Editor, Consultant, Viewer)
- Quick Access: Direct navigation to company dashboards
- Activity Feed: Recent activities across all companies

## Role Management
- Admin: Full system access and user management
- Company Admin: Complete control over company settings and users
- Consultant: Expert access with review and advisory capabilities
- Editor: Full editing permissions for products and documents
- Viewer: Read-only access for monitoring and reviews

## Multi-Company Workflow
1. Access Mission Control from the sidebar
2. Review company status and recent activities
3. Switch between companies using the dropdown
4. Navigate to specific company functions
5. Monitor compliance across your portfolio

## Best Practices
- Regularly check Mission Control for updates
- Use role-specific views to focus on relevant tasks
- Leverage the company switching feature for efficient workflows

companySetup.title: Company Setup & Configuration
companySetup.description: Initial company setup and configuration guide
companySetup.content:
# Company Setup & Configuration

Complete guide to setting up and configuring your company for optimal regulatory management.

## Initial Company Setup
1. Company Profile: Add company details, logo, and regulatory information
2. Phase Configuration: Set up custom lifecycle phases for your processes
3. Document Templates: Create reusable templates for regulatory documents
4. User Management: Invite team members and assign appropriate roles
5. Compliance Frameworks: Configure EU MDR, FDA, ISO 13485, or custom frameworks

## Phase Configuration
- Standard Phases: Concept, Design & Development, Design Transfer, Post-Market
- Custom Phases: Create company-specific phases
- Phase Gates: Define requirements and deliverables for each phase
- Milestone Tracking: Set up automatic milestone generation

## Template Management
- Document Categories: Organize templates by type (DHF, DMR, Technical Files)
- Template Libraries: Share templates across products
- Version Control: Maintain template versions and approvals
- Automated Generation: Set up automatic document creation from templates

## Compliance Framework Setup
- EU MDR Configuration: Set up annexes, classification rules, and requirements
- FDA Framework: Configure 510(k), PMA, and De Novo pathways
- ISO Standards: Implement ISO 13485, 14971, and other relevant standards
- Custom Frameworks: Create company-specific compliance requirements

productCreation.title: Product Creation & Setup
productCreation.description: Step-by-step guide to creating and setting up new products
productCreation.content:
# Product Creation & Setup

Learn how to create and configure new medical device products in the system.

## Creating a New Product
1. Basic Information: Enter product name, description, and intended use
2. Classification: Use built-in wizards for SaMD, IVDR, or EU MDR classification
3. Regulatory Pathway: Select appropriate regulatory framework (FDA, EU MDR, etc.)
4. Phase Assignment: Set initial lifecycle phase
5. Team Assignment: Add team members and define responsibilities

## Product Classification
- SaMD Classification: Software as Medical Device classification wizard
- IVDR Classification: In-vitro diagnostic regulation classification
- EU MDR Classification: Medical Device Regulation classification rules
- Risk Classification: Implement ISO 14971 risk management principles

## Initial Configuration
- Document Structure: Set up required document categories
- Milestone Definition: Define phase-specific milestones
- Compliance Requirements: Map regulatory requirements to product
- Quality System Integration: Link to QMS processes and procedures

## Best Practices
- Use descriptive product names and clear intended use statements
- Complete classification early to determine regulatory pathway
- Set up document structure before beginning development
- Regularly update product information as development progresses

productLifecycle.title: Product Lifecycle Management
productLifecycle.description: Managing products through regulatory phases and milestones
productLifecycle.content:
# Product Lifecycle Management

Comprehensive guide to managing medical devices through all lifecycle phases from concept to post-market surveillance.

## Lifecycle Phases
- Concept: Initial product planning, market research, and feasibility studies
- Design & Development: Product development, verification, and validation
- Design Transfer: Manufacturing preparation and process validation
- Post-Market Surveillance: Market monitoring and post-market studies

## Phase Management
- Phase Progression: Criteria and gates for moving between phases
- Milestone Tracking: Automatic generation and tracking of phase milestones
- Document Requirements: Phase-specific document requirements
- Review Processes: Stage-gate reviews and approvals

## Milestone System
- Automatic Generation: Milestones created based on phase and product type
- Custom Milestones: Add company-specific milestone requirements
- Progress Tracking: Visual progress indicators and completion status
- Dependencies: Link milestones to documents and deliverables

## Collaboration Features
- Team Notifications: Automatic alerts for milestone deadlines
- Review Assignments: Assign reviews to team members
- Communication Threads: Centralized discussions for each product
- Expert Consultation: Access to regulatory experts and consultants

documentControl.title: Document Control System
documentControl.description: Comprehensive document management and control processes
documentControl.content:
# Document Control System

Master the comprehensive document control system for managing regulatory documentation throughout the product lifecycle.

## Document Hierarchy
- Company Level: Templates, procedures, and standard documents
- Product Level: Product-specific documentation and deliverables
- Phase Level: Phase-specific requirements and deliverables
- Activity Level: Individual document instances and versions

## Document Types & Categories
- Design History File (DHF): Complete design and development documentation
- Device Master Record (DMR): Manufacturing documentation and procedures
- Technical Documentation: EU MDR Annex II and III requirements
- Clinical Evidence: Clinical evaluation and post-market clinical follow-up
- Risk Management: ISO 14971 risk management documentation

## Document Workflow
1. Creation: Generate documents from templates or create new
2. Draft: Initial document development and content creation
3. Under Review: Peer review and technical review processes
4. Reviewed: Review completed with comments addressed
5. Approved: Final approval and release for use

## Version Control
- Automatic Versioning: Track all document changes and revisions
- Change History: Detailed audit trail of all modifications
- Approval Workflows: Multi-level approval processes
- Distribution Control: Manage document access and distribution

templateManagement.title: Template Creation & Management
templateManagement.description: Creating and managing document templates effectively
templateManagement.content:
# Template Creation & Management

Learn to create, manage, and optimize document templates for efficient regulatory documentation.

## Template Types
- Master Templates: Core company templates for standard documents
- Product Templates: Product-specific variations and customizations
- Regulatory Templates: Framework-specific templates (EU MDR, FDA, ISO)
- Activity Templates: Templates for specific activities and deliverables

## Template Creation Process
1. Planning: Define template purpose, scope, and requirements
2. Structure: Create logical document structure and sections
3. Content: Add standard content, placeholders, and instructions
4. Formatting: Apply consistent styling and formatting
5. Review: Template review and approval process
6. Deployment: Make available for use across products

## Advanced Features
- Variable Fields: Dynamic content based on product information
- Conditional Sections: Show/hide sections based on product characteristics
- Auto-Population: Automatic insertion of product and company data
- Template Inheritance: Create template hierarchies and variations

## Best Practices
- Create comprehensive templates with clear instructions
- Use consistent formatting and structure across templates
- Regular template reviews and updates
- Training team members on proper template usage

gapAnalysis.title: Gap Analysis & Compliance Assessment
gapAnalysis.content:
# Gap Analysis & Compliance Assessment

Perform thorough gap analyses to identify and address regulatory compliance requirements.

## Gap Analysis Process
1. Scope Definition: Define assessment scope and applicable regulations
2. Requirement Mapping: Map regulatory requirements to product documentation
3. Evidence Collection: Gather and review supporting documentation
4. Gap Identification: Identify missing or insufficient evidence
5. Action Planning: Develop plans to address identified gaps

## Regulatory Frameworks
- EU MDR: Medical Device Regulation compliance assessment
- FDA Requirements: 510(k), PMA, and De Novo pathway requirements
- ISO Standards: ISO 13485, ISO 14971, IEC 62304 compliance
- IVDR: In-vitro Diagnostic Regulation requirements
- Custom Frameworks: Company-specific compliance requirements

## Assessment Categories
- Technical Documentation: Completeness and adequacy review
- Clinical Evidence: Clinical evaluation and evidence requirements
- Risk Management: ISO 14971 risk management compliance
- Quality Management: QMS integration and compliance
- Post-Market Surveillance: PMS and PMCF requirements

## Action Management
- Gap Prioritization: Rank gaps by regulatory impact and effort
- Action Assignment: Assign remediation tasks to team members
- Progress Tracking: Monitor gap closure progress and timelines
- Verification: Confirm gap closure and compliance achievement

auditManagement.title: Audit Management System
auditManagement.content:
# Audit Management System

Comprehensive audit management for regulatory inspections, internal audits, and third-party assessments.

## Audit Types
- Regulatory Inspections: FDA, Notified Body, and other regulatory audits
- Internal Audits: Company-conducted quality system audits
- Supplier Audits: Third-party and vendor assessments
- Certification Audits: ISO 13485 and other certification audits

## Audit Preparation
- Pre-Audit Planning: Prepare documentation and team assignments
- Document Reviews: Ensure all required documents are current and available
- Team Briefings: Prepare team members for audit interactions
- Facility Preparation: Organize physical spaces and resources

## During the Audit
- Finding Management: Track observations, findings, and corrective actions
- Evidence Collection: Gather and organize audit evidence
- Communication Logs: Document all audit communications and interactions
- Real-Time Updates: Update audit status and findings as they occur

## Post-Audit Activities
- Finding Analysis: Analyze audit findings and root causes
- Corrective Action Planning: Develop comprehensive corrective action plans
- Implementation Tracking: Monitor corrective action implementation
- Verification Activities: Verify effectiveness of corrective actions

classification.title: Medical Device Classification Systems
classification.description: Using classification wizards for SaMD, IVDR, and EU MDR
classification.content:
# Medical Device Classification Systems

Master the built-in classification wizards to properly classify medical devices under various regulatory frameworks.

## SaMD Classification (Software as Medical Device)
- IMDRF Framework: International Medical Device Regulators Forum guidelines
- Risk Categories: Class I, IIa, IIb, III based on healthcare situation and state
- Decision Tree: Interactive wizard guiding through classification logic
- Documentation: Automatic generation of classification rationale

## IVDR Classification (In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation)
- Annex VIII Rules: Rule-based classification system
- Risk Classes: Class A, B, C, D based on intended use and analyte
- Performance Studies: Classification impact on clinical evidence requirements
- Conformity Assessment: Notified Body involvement based on classification

## EU MDR Classification (Medical Device Regulation)
- Annex VIII Rules: 22 classification rules based on intended use
- Risk Classes: Class I, IIa, IIb, III with corresponding requirements
- Implantable Devices: Special considerations for implantable and active devices
- Combination Products: Classification of drug-device combinations

## Classification Process
1. Product Definition: Define intended use and indications for use
2. Applicable Rules: Identify applicable classification rules
3. Rule Application: Apply classification logic systematically
4. Justification: Document classification rationale and evidence
5. Review: Expert review of classification decisions

userManagement.title: User Management & Permissions
userManagement.description: Managing team members, roles, and access permissions
userManagement.content:
# User Management & Permissions

Comprehensive guide to managing team members, roles, and access permissions within the platform.

## User Roles & Permissions
- Admin: Full system access, user management, and global settings
- Company Admin: Complete control over company settings and users
- Consultant: Expert access with review and advisory capabilities
- Editor: Full editing permissions for products and documents
- Viewer: Read-only access for monitoring and reviews

## User Invitation Process
1. Invite Users: Send invitations with appropriate role assignments
2. Role Selection: Choose appropriate role based on responsibilities
3. Company Association: Associate users with specific companies
4. Access Configuration: Configure feature-specific access permissions
5. Onboarding: Provide training and orientation materials

## Role-Based Access Control
- Feature Access: Control access to specific platform features
- Data Permissions: Manage read/write access to documents and products
- Company Boundaries: Restrict access to appropriate companies
- Functional Areas: Control access to specific functional areas

## Best Practices
- Implement least privilege principle for access control
- Regular review and cleanup of user permissions
- Document role definitions and responsibilities clearly
- Provide adequate training for each role type
- Monitor user activity for security and compliance

businessAnalysis.title: Business Analysis & NPV Tools
businessAnalysis.description: Using business analysis tools for commercial planning
businessAnalysis.content:
# Business Analysis & NPV Tools

Leverage built-in business analysis tools for commercial planning and investment decision-making.

## NPV Analysis (Net Present Value)
- Cash Flow Modeling: Model development costs, revenues, and ongoing expenses
- Risk Assessment: Incorporate uncertainty and risk factors into financial models
- Scenario Analysis: Compare different development and commercialization scenarios
- Sensitivity Analysis: Understand key drivers of project economics

## Commercial Planning
- Market Analysis: Assess market size, growth, and competitive landscape
- Regulatory Pathway: Model regulatory costs and timelines
- Development Costs: Track actual vs. planned development expenditures
- Revenue Projections: Model sales forecasts and pricing strategies

## Business Case Development
1. Market Opportunity: Define target market and value proposition
2. Technical Feasibility: Assess technical risks and development requirements
3. Regulatory Strategy: Plan regulatory pathway and associated costs
4. Financial Projections: Develop comprehensive financial models
5. Risk Assessment: Identify and quantify key business risks
6. Decision Framework: Create clear go/no-go decision criteria

## Integration with Product Development
- Phase Gate Reviews: Integrate business analysis into development gates
- Resource Planning: Optimize resource allocation across projects
- Portfolio Management: Manage portfolio of development projects
- Strategic Alignment: Ensure projects align with business strategy

communications.title: Communications & Collaboration
communications.content:
# Communications & Collaboration

Effective team communication and collaboration using built-in communication tools.

## Communication Channels
- Product Discussions: Dedicated communication threads for each product
- Company-Wide Updates: Broadcast important announcements and updates
- Project Teams: Focused communication for specific project teams
- Expert Consultations: Direct access to regulatory experts and consultants

## Activity Tracking
- Recent Activities: Monitor all recent changes and updates
- User Actions: Track who made what changes and when
- Document Changes: Detailed change logs for all document modifications
- Milestone Updates: Track progress on milestones and deliverables

## Collaboration Features
- Threaded Discussions: Organize conversations by topic and context
- File Sharing: Share documents and files within communication threads
- Notifications: Configurable notifications for important events
- @Mentions: Direct attention to specific team members

## Review & Approval Processes
- Review Assignments: Assign specific reviews to team members
- Approval Workflows: Multi-level approval processes for critical decisions
- Comment Systems: Detailed commenting on documents and deliverables
- Resolution Tracking: Track comment resolution and approval status

## Communication Management
- Thread Organization: Organize communications by product, phase, or topic
- Search & Filter: Find specific communications and discussions quickly
- Archive Management: Archive completed discussions and communications
- Reporting: Generate communication reports and activity summaries

## Best Practices
- Use descriptive thread titles and clear communication
- Tag relevant team members for important discussions
- Document decisions and action items clearly
- Regular communication to keep teams aligned
- Archive completed discussions to maintain organization

billing.title: Billing & Subscription Management
billing.description: Managing billing, subscriptions, and plan features
billing.content:
# Billing & Subscription Management

Manage your subscription plans, billing, and access to premium features.

## Subscription Plans
- Starter Plan: Basic features for small teams and single products
- Professional Plan: Enhanced features for growing companies
- Business Plan: Advanced features for established organizations
- Enterprise Plan: Full feature access with custom integrations

## Plan Features
- User Limits: Number of team members per plan
- Product Limits: Maximum number of products per company
- Document Storage: Storage limits and archival options
- Advanced Features: Access to specialized tools and integrations
- Support Levels: Different levels of customer support

## Billing Management
- Payment Methods: Credit card, invoicing, and other payment options
- Billing Cycles: Monthly, annual, and custom billing arrangements
- Usage Monitoring: Track usage against plan limits
- Cost Center Allocation: Allocate costs to specific departments or projects

## Plan Upgrades & Changes
- Upgrade Process: Seamless upgrade to higher-tier plans
- Feature Activation: Immediate access to new features upon upgrade
- Downgrade Considerations: Data retention and feature limitations
- Custom Plans: Enterprise-level custom plan negotiations

archiveManagement.title: Archive & Data Management
archiveManagement.description: Managing archived products and historical data
archiveManagement.content:
# Archive & Data Management

Comprehensive guide to managing archived products, historical data, and long-term data retention.

## Archive System
- Product Archiving: Archive completed or discontinued products
- Document Retention: Long-term document storage and retrieval
- Data Preservation: Maintain data integrity over extended periods
- Access Control: Manage access to archived information

## Archiving Process
1. Archive Preparation: Prepare products and documents for archiving
2. Data Validation: Verify completeness and integrity of archived data
3. Access Configuration: Set appropriate access permissions for archived content
4. Retention Policies: Apply appropriate data retention policies
5. Archive Completion: Finalize archiving process and documentation

## Archive Access & Retrieval
- Search Functionality: Find archived products and documents quickly
- Restoration Process: Restore archived products when needed
- Audit Trails: Maintain complete audit trails for archived content
- Export Capabilities: Export archived data for external use

## Compliance Considerations
- Regulatory Requirements: Meet regulatory data retention requirements
- Audit Preparation: Prepare archived data for regulatory audits
- Data Integrity: Ensure data integrity throughout retention period
- Privacy Protection: Protect sensitive information in archived data

regulatory.description: Assesses regulatory pathway clarity, precedent availability, and compliance risks. Factor in device class, clinical data requirements, and market authorization timelines.

commercial.description: Measures market opportunity, competitive positioning, and revenue potential. Consider reimbursement pathways, pricing power, and sales channel access.

viabilityScorecard.tip: Be honest in your assessments. Investors value realistic projections over optimistic ones. Document specific risk factors and mitigation strategies for each dimension.

sam.description: The portion of TAM you can realistically target given your geographic focus, regulatory approvals, and product fit.

som.title: Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM)
som.description: The realistic market share you can capture in 3-5 years given competition and go-to-market strategy.

practices.step1: Use both top-down (market reports) and bottom-up (patient counts × ASP) approaches
practices.step2: Cite credible sources: peer-reviewed literature, epidemiology databases, industry reports
practices.step3: Show your math - investors want to see the assumptions
practices.step4: Be conservative - overinflated markets signal inexperience

marketSizing.tip: The Volume × ASP validation helps ensure your monetary estimates align with patient population data. Significant mismatches may indicate flawed assumptions.

eudamed.description: EUDAMED is the European Database on Medical Devices - a mandatory registration system for EU MDR compliance.
eudamed.whatIsDescription: EUDAMED (European Database on Medical Devices) is the EU's centralized IT system for:

timeline.may2026Desc: MANDATORY registration for ALL device classes (III, IIb, IIa, I)
timeline.nov2026Desc: Deadline for Legacy Devices (devices under old MDD certificates)

eudamed.regulationNote: Based on Regulation (EU) 2024/1860 and Commission Decision November 2025
eudamed.mdrDeadlinesDescription: These are when your device must be fully MDR certified, not just registered in EUDAMED:

mdr.may2024Desc: Required QMS in place + Notified Body application lodged for extension eligibility
mdr.dec2027Desc: Class III & Class IIb Implantables must be MDR Certified
mdr.dec2028Desc: Class IIb, IIa, and Class I (sterile/measuring/reusable) must be MDR Certified

actions.getSrnDesc: You need a Single Registration Number to apply to a Notified Body. Cannot wait for mandatory deadline.
actions.assignUdiDesc: Must be assigned before you apply for your CE mark, even if you don't upload to EUDAMED yet.
actions.prepareDataDesc: Have your UDI data ready in Xyreg for seamless submission when required.

eudamed.actorRegistrationDesc: To register as an Economic Operator and receive your Single Registration Number (SRN), you need:

euLogin.description: Create an EU Login account for your organization. This is the authentication gateway for all EU systems.

prrc.title: Person Responsible for Regulatory Compliance (PRRC)
prrc.description: Designate a qualified PRRC with documented expertise in regulatory affairs. Required under MDR Article 15.

legalEntity.title: Legal Entity Information
legalEntity.description: Company legal name, registered address, VAT number, and contact details.

authRep.title: Authorised Representative (if non-EU)

eudamed.actorTimeline: ⏱️ Timeline: Actor registration typically takes 4-6 weeks including verification.

steps.step1: Create EU Login credentials and register your organization
steps.step2: Complete Actor Registration form and receive your SRN (4-6 weeks)
steps.step3: Assign Basic UDI-DIs in Xyreg for each device family
steps.step4: Prepare UDI data using XML format or EUDAMED manual entry
steps.step5: Submit Basic UDI-DI for each device family
steps.step6: Register individual UDI-DIs with complete attributes
steps.step7: Link devices to Notified Body certificates
steps.step8: Verify data accuracy and publish to public database

eudamed.tip: Act now, don't wait! Get your SRN immediately - it's required before any Notified Body application. The May 2026 EUDAMED deadline applies to ALL device classes simultaneously. Use Xyreg's UDI Management to prepare your data now for seamless EUDAMED submission.

companyMilestones.description: Company Milestones provide a consolidated view of key dates and deliverables across all products and company-level activities.

productMilestones.title: Product Milestones
productMilestones.description: Development phase gates, regulatory submissions, and launch dates for individual devices.

companyMilestones.title: Company Milestones
companyMilestones.description: Organization-wide events like QMS certifications, facility audits, and strategic initiatives.

regulatoryDeadlines.title: Regulatory Deadlines
regulatoryDeadlines.description: Submission deadlines, renewal dates, and compliance milestones across all markets.

timelineSteps.step1: Set target dates for each milestone aligned with business objectives
timelineSteps.step3: Track progress and update status regularly
timelineSteps.step4: Use the calendar view for resource planning across projects

companyMilestones.tip: Link product milestones to company-level objectives to ensure strategic alignment and resource coordination.

companyDocuments.description: Company Documents manages your organization-level QMS documentation including SOPs, policies, and controlled records.

sops.title: Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)
sops.description: Company-wide procedures for quality, regulatory, and operational processes.

policies.title: Policies
policies.description: High-level organizational policies governing quality, safety, and compliance.

templates.title: Templates & Forms
templates.description: Standardized templates used across products and projects.

records.title: Records
records.description: Completed forms, reports, and evidence of compliance activities.

controlSteps.step1: Create or upload documents with proper metadata
controlSteps.step2: Route for review and approval per your workflow
controlSteps.step3: Publish approved documents and notify stakeholders
controlSteps.step4: Manage revisions with full version history
controlSteps.step5: Archive obsolete documents while maintaining traceability

companyDocuments.tip: Use document templates to ensure consistency and reduce time creating new controlled documents.

companyAudits.description: The Audit Management module helps you plan, execute, and track internal and external audits for QMS compliance.

internal.title: Internal Audits
internal.description: Self-assessments to verify QMS effectiveness and identify improvement opportunities.

supplier.title: Supplier Audits
supplier.description: Assessments of critical suppliers per ISO 13485 requirements.

notifiedBody.title: Notified Body Audits
notifiedBody.description: External audits for CE marking certification and surveillance.

regulatory.title: Regulatory Inspections
regulatory.description: FDA inspections and other regulatory authority assessments.

workflowSteps.step1: Schedule audits in the annual audit program
workflowSteps.step5: Create CAPAs for findings requiring corrective action
workflowSteps.step6: Track CAPA closure and verify effectiveness

companyAudits.tip: Maintain an annual audit schedule covering all QMS processes. ISO 13485 requires that the entire QMS be audited over a defined period.

companyTraining.description: Human Resources covers Clause 6.2 (Competence, Awareness & Training) — managing roles, competencies, and training records beyond just training modules.

modules.title: Training Modules
modules.description: Define training content including SOPs, work instructions, and external certifications.

roleBased.title: Role-Based Assignment
roleBased.description: Automatically assign required training based on job roles and responsibilities.

competency.title: Competency Tracking
competency.description: Track completion status, due dates, and training effectiveness.

features.step1: Create training modules linked to SOPs and procedures
features.step3: Auto-assign training when users are assigned roles
features.step4: Track completion and send reminders for overdue training
features.step5: Re-issue training when documents are updated

companyTraining.tip: ISO 13485 requires documented evidence of training effectiveness. Use assessments or practical demonstrations to verify competency.

companySuppliers.description: Supplier Management helps you qualify, monitor, and control suppliers of materials, components, and services critical to product quality.

qualification.title: Qualification
qualification.description: Assess supplier capability through audits, questionnaires, and performance history.

approvedList.title: Approved Supplier List
approvedList.description: Maintain a controlled list of approved suppliers with qualification status.

performance.title: Performance Monitoring
performance.description: Track quality metrics, delivery performance, and non-conformance rates.

risk.title: Risk Assessment
risk.description: Evaluate supplier criticality and implement appropriate controls.

companySuppliers.tip: The level of supplier control should be proportional to the risk and criticality of the supplied product or service to your device quality.

companyPMS.description: Post-Market Surveillance (PMS) is the systematic process of collecting and analyzing safety and performance data after your device is on the market.

complaints.title: Complaint Handling
complaints.description: Receive, investigate, and trend customer complaints and feedback.

vigilance.title: Vigilance Reporting
vigilance.description: Report serious incidents and field safety corrective actions to authorities.

psur.title: Periodic Safety Update Reports
psur.description: Compile and submit PSURs as required by EU MDR timelines.

pmcf.title: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up
pmcf.description: Proactively collect clinical data to confirm ongoing safety and performance.

companyPMS.classIII: Class III and implantables: Annual PSUR
companyPMS.classII: Class IIa and IIb: PSUR every 2 years
companyPMS.classI: Class I: Update PMS report as needed
companyPMS.tip: PMS data feeds back into risk management and design improvements. Establish clear processes to route PMS findings to appropriate teams.

companyPortfolio.description: Portfolio Management provides a strategic overview of your product portfolio including development pipeline, market performance, and resource allocation.

pipeline.title: Pipeline Overview
pipeline.description: Visualize products across development phases from concept to market.

market.title: Market Coverage
market.description: See regulatory status and market authorization across global markets.

financial.title: Financial Analysis
financial.description: Review revenue, rNPV projections, and resource investment by product.

companyPortfolio.tip: Use portfolio analytics to inform go/no-go decisions and prioritize resource allocation across competing projects.

riskManagement.description: Risk Management per ISO 14971 is essential for medical device safety and regulatory compliance. The Hazard Traceability Matrix documents your systematic approach to identifying, assessing, and controlling risks.

hazardId.title: 1. Hazard Identification
hazardId.description: Identify all foreseeable hazards associated with your device in both normal and fault conditions. Consider energy hazards, biological hazards, environmental hazards, and use errors.

riskEstimation.title: 2. Risk Estimation
riskEstimation.description: Estimate the probability of occurrence and severity of harm for each hazard. Use your risk matrix (e.g., 5×5 severity vs. probability) to determine initial risk levels.

riskEvaluation.title: 3. Risk Evaluation
riskEvaluation.description: Determine if risk reduction is required by comparing estimated risks against acceptability criteria. Unacceptable risks must be mitigated.

riskControl.title: 4. Risk Control
riskControl.description: Implement controls in priority order: (1) Inherent safety by design, (2) Protective measures, (3) Information for safety (IFU warnings).

residualRisk.title: 5. Residual Risk Evaluation
residualRisk.description: Re-assess risks after controls are implemented. Verify controls are effective and don't introduce new hazards.

riskManagement.unacceptableDesc: Risks that must be reduced regardless of cost
riskManagement.alarpDesc: As Low As Reasonably Practicable - reduce if benefits outweigh costs
riskManagement.acceptableDesc: Risks that are tolerable without additional measures
riskManagement.investorDescription: A comprehensive risk management file demonstrates regulatory maturity and reduces investment risk. Poorly managed device risks can lead to recalls, liability, and regulatory delays - all of which destroy shareholder value.
riskManagement.tip: Link hazards to your design requirements and verification tests. This traceability demonstrates that your design controls systematically address identified risks - a key audit expectation.

whyInvestors.title: Why Investors Need This Information
whyInvestors.description: MedTech investors evaluate opportunities based on specific criteria that align with their due diligence requirements.

marketOpportunity.title: Market Opportunity
marketOpportunity.description: Is the clinical need real? Is the market large enough to justify the investment?

executionCapability.title: Execution Capability
executionCapability.description: Does your team have the expertise to navigate regulatory and commercial challenges?

riskProfile.title: Risk Profile
riskProfile.description: Are regulatory, clinical, and commercial risks understood and mitigated?

guidedJourney.title: Your Guided Journey
guidedJourney.description: Each step in the checklist guides you to the relevant module where you can complete the required information.

amberPanel.title: Look for the amber panel
amberPanel.description: On the right side of each page, you'll see "To Complete This Step" with specific fields to fill in.

builtOnWork.title: Built on Your Existing Work
builtOnWork.description: You're not creating a separate pitch deck from scratch. Genesis pulls from the work you've already done in XyReg.

genesisIntro.everyFieldContributes: Every field you've filled in XyReg contributes to your investor readiness.
genesisIntro.doesntDuplicate: Genesis doesn't duplicate your work—it organizes it for investor consumption.

overview.title: Smart Cost Intelligence Overview
overview.description: AI-powered cost estimation and market analysis
overview.content:
# Smart Cost Intelligence System

The Smart Cost Intelligence system provides AI-powered cost estimation for medical device market extensions.

## Key Features
- Market-Specific Cost Templates
- Device Complexity Intelligence
- Scenario Planning (Conservative/Typical/Aggressive)
- Smart Adjustments (currency, inflation)

multipliers.title: Device Class Cost Multipliers
multipliers.description: How device complexity affects regulatory costs
multipliers.content:
# Device Class Cost Multipliers

The system adjusts costs based on regulatory classification.

- Class I: 1.0x base
- Class II: 1.2x base (510(k))
- Class III: 1.8x base (PMA)
- IVD: Variable 1.1–2.0x
- SaMD: Variable 1.0–1.8x

missionControl.description: Mission Control is your personal command center. Everything on this page is scoped to you and your company — what needs your attention, what changed recently, and who is trying to reach you.

actionItems.title: My Action Items
actionItems.subtitle: Your personal task list with two internal tabs:

approvals.title: Approvals
approvals.description: Items waiting for your sign-off — document approvals, activity confirmations, and quality reviews assigned to you. Each card shows the item name, product, and how long it has been waiting.

deadlines.title: Deadlines
deadlines.description: Overdue documents and upcoming audits. Documents past their due date appear in red; audits approaching within 30 days appear in amber. Click any item to jump directly to it.

actionItems.tip: Review action items first thing each morning. Approvals blocking other team members should take priority.

activityStream.title: Activity Stream
activityStream.description: A chronological feed of the most recent changes across your company — product updates, document uploads, task completions, and status changes. Each entry shows what changed, which product it belongs to, and when it happened.
activityStream.tip: Use the Activity Stream to stay informed without checking each module individually. It shows the last 15 events.

competencyRadar.title: My Competency Radar
competencyRadar.description: Your personal training compliance tracker. It pulls from the company's training records to show which SOPs, training modules, and competency requirements you have completed versus what is still pending. Items are sorted by urgency — overdue trainings appear first.
competencyRadar.tip: Keeping your competency radar green is important for audit readiness. Overdue trainings can flag compliance gaps during inspections.

communicationHub.title: Communication Hub
communicationHub.subtitle: Internal messaging scoped to your company. It shows:

unread.title: Unread Messages & Priority Threads
unread.description: Messages you haven't read yet appear at the top with a count badge. High-priority threads are highlighted so you don't miss urgent conversations.

composer.title: Quick Message Composer
composer.description: Send a message to any team member without leaving Mission Control. Select a recipient, type your message, and send — all within the dashboard.

communicationHub.tip: Check the Communication Hub at the start and end of each day to stay on top of team discussions.

genesisOverview.description: Learn how to prepare your investor-ready business case using XyReg Genesis.

targetMarkets.description: Select the geographic markets where you plan to launch your device. Each market has different regulatory requirements, classification rules, and timelines.

euMdr.title: European Union (EU MDR)
euMdr.description: CE marking required. Classification per Annex VIII rules. Notified Body involvement for Class IIa and above.

fda.title: United States (FDA)
fda.description: 510(k) clearance for Class II, PMA for Class III. Predicate device strategy critical for 510(k) pathway.

ukca.title: United Kingdom (UKCA)
ukca.description: Post-Brexit, UK requires separate UKCA marking and UK Approved Body. Transition period rules may apply.

targetMarkets.tip: Select target markets early - they drive your regulatory strategy, clinical evidence requirements, and development timeline.

economicBuyer.description: The Economic Buyer is the person or entity with budget authority to purchase your device. Understanding their needs is critical for commercial success.

budget.title: Who Controls the Budget?
budget.description: Hospital administrator, department head, GPO, or individual physician? Different buyers have different decision criteria.

procurement.title: Procurement Path
procurement.description: Direct purchase, GPO contract, capital equipment committee, or value analysis committee? Understand the buying process.

budgetCycle.title: Budget Cycle
budgetCycle.description: When are purchasing decisions made? Align your sales cycle with the buyer's fiscal year and budget planning.

economicBuyer.tip: The user (physician) and buyer (administrator) often have different priorities. Your value proposition must address both.

userProfile.description: Define who uses your device, in what context, and why. This information drives regulatory labeling, training requirements, and clinical evidence needs.

patient.title: Patient Population
patient.description: Who benefits from the device? Define demographics, conditions, inclusion/exclusion criteria.

operators.title: Intended Operators
operators.description: Healthcare professionals, trained technicians, patients, or caregivers? Affects training and IFU requirements.

environment.title: Environment of Use
environment.description: Hospital, clinic, home, ambulatory? Affects design requirements and use error analysis.

duration.title: Duration of Use
duration.description: Single procedure, short-term therapy, or chronic use? Impacts classification and clinical evidence.

userProfile.tip: User profile information appears in regulatory submissions and investor materials. Be specific and evidence-based.

portfolioOverview.description: The Portfolio Financial Health view provides leading indicators for strategic decision-making across your entire product development portfolio.

rnpv.title: Risk-Adjusted NPV (rNPV)
rnpv.description: Portfolio value adjusted for probability of success. Uses weighted cash flows by phase and discount rate.

valueAtRisk.title: Value at Risk
valueAtRisk.description: Quantifies potential portfolio losses. Helps identify concentration risks and vulnerability to project failures.

riskScore.title: Portfolio Risk Score
riskScore.description: Aggregate risk level (Value at Risk / Total Value). Lower is better—indicates more resilient portfolio.

rnpvFormula.title: Risk-Adjusted NPV
rnpvFormula.explanation: Sum of cash flows (CF) at each time period (t), weighted by Probability of Success (PoS) and discounted at rate (r).

riskFormula.title: Portfolio Risk Score
riskFormula.formula: Risk Score = (Value at Risk / Total Portfolio Value) × 100
riskFormula.explanation: Higher scores indicate concentrated risk. Target: below 25% for balanced portfolios.

navigation.title: Quick Navigation
navigation.projectHealth: Project Health Metrics → Individual project deep-dive
navigation.operational: Operational Health → QMS and process metrics

portfolioOverview.tip: Use portfolio filters to analyze specific categories, platforms, or lifecycle phases. Trends showing consistent decline warrant immediate strategic review.

projectHealth.description: Real-time leading indicators for project performance. These metrics predict delays before they impact milestones.

testVelocity.title: Test Velocity
testVelocity.description: Rate of test execution and pass rate. Higher velocity with stable pass rates indicates healthy development.

reqVolatility.title: Requirement Volatility
reqVolatility.description: Measures requirements changes over time. High volatility late in development is a risk indicator.

approvalTime.title: Avg Approval Time
approvalTime.description: Average time for document approvals. Bottlenecks here delay entire development phases.

activeRisks.title: Active Risks
activeRisks.description: Open risk items across the portfolio. Monitors trend vs peak to identify emerging issues.

velocity.title: Test Velocity
velocity.explanation: Rolling weekly average. Compare vs low point to track improvement. Target: stable or increasing trend.

volatility.title: Requirement Volatility
volatility.explanation: Measures instability. Compare vs peak month. Target: <10% in late phases.

approvalCycle.title: Approval Cycle Time
approvalCycle.formula: Avg Time = Σ (Approval Date - Submission Date) / Count
approvalCycle.explanation: Average days from document submission to final approval. Identifies process bottlenecks.

statusIndicators.title: Status Indicators
statusIndicators.onTarget: Metrics within healthy ranges, no action needed

projectHealth.tip: Trends matter more than snapshots. A gradual decline in test velocity often precedes schedule slips by 2-4 weeks.

operationalHealth.description: Quality Management System health indicators. Monitors CAPA effectiveness, supplier risk, reviewer workload, and complaint trends.

healthIndex.title: QMS Health Index
healthIndex.description: Composite score (0-100%) measuring overall quality system responsiveness and effectiveness.

supplierRisk.title: Supplier Risk
supplierRisk.description: Count of high-risk suppliers requiring close monitoring. Based on audit scores and delivery performance.

reviewerBottleneck.title: Reviewer Bottleneck
reviewerBottleneck.description: Number of pending reviews per reviewer. Identifies workload imbalances that delay approvals.

criticalComplaints.title: Critical Complaints
criticalComplaints.description: High-severity field issues trending up. Early warning for safety or regulatory concerns.

calculations.title: How QMS Health Index Is Calculated

healthIndexFormula.title: QMS Health Index (0-100%)
healthIndexFormula.explanation: Weighted composite of three sub-scores. Higher is better. Target: ≥75% for healthy QMS.

calculations.capaScore: CAPA Score: (Closed on-time CAPAs / Total CAPAs) × 100
calculations.auditScore: Audit Score: Based on open findings: 0 critical = 100, 1+ critical = scaled down

supplierFormula.title: Supplier Risk Score
supplierFormula.formula: Risk Level = f(Audit Score, Delivery Rate, Critical Issues)
supplierFormula.explanation: Suppliers with audit scores <70, delivery <90%, or open CAPAs are flagged high-risk.

bottleneckFormula.title: Reviewer Bottleneck
bottleneckFormula.explanation: Identifies the reviewer with highest queue. Target: <15 reviews per person.

trendInterpretation.title: QMS Health Trend Interpretation
trendInterpretation.atRisk: At Risk - Declining closure rates, investigate causes
trendInterpretation.critical: Critical - Systemic issues, immediate intervention needed

regulatoryContext.title: Regulatory Context
regulatoryContext.iso13485: ISO 13485:2016 requires effective QMS monitoring and continual improvement.
regulatoryContext.qmsr: QMSR (21 CFR 820) emphasizes CAPA effectiveness and production controls.
regulatoryContext.euMdr: EU MDR requires documented evidence of QMS performance for Notified Body audits.

operationalHealth.tip: A declining QMS Health Index often precedes audit findings by 3-6 months. Address root causes early to avoid regulatory observations.

advancedFinancial.description: Operational efficiency and resource utilization metrics. Tracks process throughput, resource allocation, and cost efficiency.

throughput.title: Process Throughput
throughput.description: Documents and activities completed per time period. Measures team productivity.

utilization.title: Resource Utilization
utilization.description: Allocation efficiency across projects. Identifies over/under-utilized teams.

costPerMilestone.title: Cost per Milestone
costPerMilestone.description: Average spend to achieve key deliverables. Benchmarks against industry standards.

onTimeDelivery.title: On-Time Delivery Rate
onTimeDelivery.description: Percentage of milestones completed by target date. Leading indicator of project health.

throughputFormula.title: Process Throughput
throughputFormula.explanation: Rolling 30-day count. Compare vs baseline to identify productivity changes.

utilizationFormula.title: Resource Utilization
utilizationFormula.explanation: Target: 70-85%. Below 70% = underutilized, above 85% = burnout risk.

efficiencyFormula.title: Cost Efficiency Ratio
efficiencyFormula.explanation: Ratio <1.0 = under budget, >1.0 = over budget. Track trends over time.

scenarioPlanning.title: Scenario Planning
scenarioPlanning.description: The efficiency view supports scenario analysis:
scenarioPlanning.baseCase: Base Case: Current trajectory and resource allocation

advancedFinancial.tip: Balance efficiency with quality. High throughput with declining quality metrics (defect rates, rework) may indicate unsustainable pace.

valueProposition.description: Your value proposition articulates the measurable benefit your device delivers. Investors and buyers need to understand the ROI clearly.

clinical.title: Clinical Outcomes
clinical.description: What patient outcomes improve? Quantify with metrics: survival rates, complication reduction, recovery time.

economic.title: Economic Value
economic.description: What costs are reduced? Procedure time, hospital stay, readmissions, consumables, staff time.

operational.title: Operational Value
operational.description: What workflow improvements result? Efficiency gains, training reduction, integration benefits.

valueProposition.tip: "Better outcomes at lower cost" is the ideal value proposition. Quantify both dimensions with real data or credible estimates.

ipStrategy.description: Intellectual property creates your competitive moat. This page covers your proprietary IP and Freedom to Operate (FTO).

patents.title: Patents
patents.description: Utility patents protect innovations for 20 years. Cover core technology, manufacturing processes, and key features.

tradeSecrets.title: Trade Secrets
tradeSecrets.description: Confidential know-how that provides competitive advantage. Unlike patents, trade secrets never expire but require active protection.

trademarks.title: Trademarks
trademarks.description: Protect brand names, logos, and product names. Registration provides nationwide priority.

designRights.title: Design Rights
designRights.description: Protect the ornamental appearance of products. Similar to patents but protect visual design rather than function.

copyrights.title: Copyrights
copyrights.description: Protect original creative works like software code, documentation, and marketing materials.

fto.title: Freedom to Operate (FTO)
fto.description: FTO assessment determines whether you can commercialize without infringing others' patents.

certainty.title: FTO Certainty Level
certainty.description: How confident is your FTO assessment? Ranges from 'Uncertain' to 'Certain' (legal opinion obtained).

status.title: FTO Status
status.description: 'Full FTO' means no blocking patents found. 'Limited/No FTO' requires a mitigation strategy.

mitigation.title: Mitigation Strategy
mitigation.description: If FTO is limited: license acquisition, design-around, patent challenges, or geographic restrictions.

ownership.title: IP Ownership
ownership.description: Investors scrutinize IP ownership during due diligence. Ensure clean assignment from founders, employees, and contractors.

ipStrategy.warning: Unaddressed FTO issues are deal-breakers for investors. Assess FTO before commercialization.
ipStrategy.tip: Strong IP is both protection and competitive advantage. Investors view patents as barriers to entry.

useOfProceeds.description: Show investors exactly how their capital will be deployed to achieve your next milestones.

rd.title: R&D & Engineering
rd.description: Product development, prototyping, design verification, software development.

regulatory.title: Regulatory & Clinical
regulatory.description: Regulatory submissions, clinical trials, testing, certifications.

operations.title: Operations & Manufacturing
operations.description: Manufacturing setup, supply chain, quality systems, inventory.

commercial.title: Commercial & Team
commercial.description: Sales, marketing, key hires, G&A expenses.

useOfProceeds.tip: Align use of proceeds with specific milestones. Investors want to see capital efficiency and clear value inflection points.

gtmStrategy.description: Your go-to-market strategy defines how you'll reach customers and generate revenue.

sales.title: Sales Model
sales.description: Direct sales, distributors, GPO contracts, or hybrid? Match to your market and deal size.

pricing.title: Pricing Strategy
pricing.description: Premium, value-based, or competitive pricing? Align with reimbursement reality.

launch.title: Launch Markets
launch.description: Which territories first? Often lead with fastest regulatory pathway and strongest reimbursement.

acquisition.title: Customer Acquisition
acquisition.description: KOL strategy, pilot sites, case studies, conference presence, digital marketing.

gtmStrategy.tip: Regulatory approval is not commercial success. Show investors you understand the path from clearance to revenue.

manufacturing.description: Manufacturing strategy affects cost, quality, and scalability. Investors need confidence in your production capability.

makeVsBuy.title: Make vs. Buy
makeVsBuy.description: In-house vs. contract manufacturing? Consider volume, margins, IP sensitivity, and capital.

supplier.title: Supplier Strategy
supplier.description: Single-source vs. dual-source for critical components? Balance cost with supply chain risk.

quality.title: Quality System
quality.description: ISO 13485 certified? FDA registered? Manufacturing QMS requirements for medical devices.

scaleUp.title: Scale-up Plan
scaleUp.description: How will you scale from prototype to commercial volumes? Identify capacity constraints early.

manufacturing.tip: Manufacturing problems can delay launch and erode margins. Show investors you've thought through production strategy.

exitStrategy.description: Investors need to see a path to liquidity. Show potential acquirers and realistic valuation expectations.

acquirers.title: Strategic Acquirers
acquirers.description: Which large medtech companies would benefit from your technology? Consider strategic fit and M&A history.

comparables.title: Comparable Transactions
comparables.description: What have similar companies sold for? Revenue multiples, milestone payments, deal structures.

valueDrivers.title: Value Drivers
valueDrivers.description: What makes you attractive to acquirers? Regulatory approval, clinical data, revenue, IP, team.

timeline.title: Timeline to Exit
timeline.description: When would an exit likely occur? Typically 5-7 years from seed, aligned with value inflection points.

exitStrategy.tip: Medtech exits are typically M&A to strategic acquirers, not IPO. Know who buys companies like yours.

strategicBlueprint.description: The Strategic Blueprint is your organization's long-term commercial strategy document. It defines where you compete, how you win, and what milestones indicate progress toward strategic goals.

vision.title: Vision & Mission
vision.description: Your company's strategic direction and purpose. What problem are you solving at the organizational level?

positioning.title: Market Positioning
positioning.description: Where do you sit in the competitive landscape? Technology leader, cost leader, niche specialist, or full-solution provider?

growth.title: Growth Vectors
growth.description: Organic growth (new products, new markets) vs. inorganic growth (acquisitions, partnerships, licensing).

kpis.title: Strategic KPIs
kpis.description: Measurable targets that track progress: revenue growth rate, market share, gross margin, portfolio diversity.

strategicBlueprint.tip: Review and update the Strategic Blueprint annually. Link it to your Management Review process to ensure strategic and quality objectives stay aligned.

commercialIntelligence.description: Commercial Intelligence is your strategic command center for enterprise-level business planning. Unlike device-level business cases (which evaluate individual products), this module looks across your entire portfolio to optimize commercial strategy.

blueprint.title: Strategic Blueprint
blueprint.description: Long-term strategic planning with market positioning, growth vectors, and competitive analysis at the portfolio level.

canvas.title: Business Canvas
canvas.description: Lean canvas framework applied at the organizational level — your company's overall value proposition, channels, and revenue model.

market.title: Market Analysis
market.description: Cross-portfolio market sizing, competitive landscape, and market share analysis across all target geographies.

performance.title: Commercial Performance
performance.description: Revenue tracking, margin analysis, and KPI dashboards for the entire product portfolio.

pricing.title: Pricing Strategy
pricing.description: Enterprise pricing framework, cross-product pricing consistency, and reimbursement optimization.

access.title: Global Market Access
access.description: Country-by-country market entry strategy, registration pathways, and regulatory requirements by geography.

commercialIntelligence.tip: Use Commercial Intelligence at the enterprise level to make portfolio allocation decisions — which products to invest in, which markets to enter, and where to optimize pricing.

qmsFoundation.title: What is the QMS Foundation?
qmsFoundation.description: The QMS Foundation represents your company-level Quality Management System infrastructure — the "walls of the factory" that support all device development activities.

enterpriseMarketAnalysis.description: Enterprise Market Analysis provides cross-portfolio market intelligence. While device-level analysis sizes individual product markets, this view reveals portfolio-level patterns — which markets have the best overall opportunity, where you're over/under-indexed, and how competitive dynamics shift across geographies.

marketMap.title: Portfolio Market Map
marketMap.description: Geographic heatmap showing revenue potential across all products by region. Identifies underserved markets with growth opportunity.

competitive.title: Competitive Landscape
competitive.description: Cross-product competitive analysis — which competitors appear across multiple product categories and how concentrated is the market?

growth.title: Market Growth Trends
growth.description: Industry-level growth rates by therapeutic area and geography, overlaid with your portfolio positioning.

enterpriseMarketAnalysis.tip: Use this module to identify portfolio gaps — therapy areas or geographies where you have no product presence but market opportunity exists.

commercialPerformance.description: Commercial Performance tracks revenue, margins, and key business metrics across your entire product portfolio. This is the financial dashboard for commercial leadership — showing how each product and market contributes to organizational performance.

revenue.title: Revenue by Product
revenue.description: Monthly and annual revenue for each device, with year-over-year growth rates and variance against forecast.

margin.title: Margin Analysis
margin.description: Gross and contribution margin by product line. Identify which products are most profitable and which need pricing or cost optimization.

marketLevel.title: Market-Level Performance
marketLevel.description: Revenue and unit sales by geography, revealing which markets deliver the strongest returns.

cac.title: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)
cac.description: How much does it cost to acquire a new customer for each product line? Compare against lifetime value for unit economics.

commercialPerformance.tip: Review commercial performance monthly with your leadership team. Use it to make portfolio investment decisions — double down on winners, fix or divest underperformers.

pricingStrategy.description: Enterprise Pricing Strategy ensures pricing consistency and optimization across your product portfolio. In MedTech, pricing decisions are constrained by reimbursement codes, group purchasing organizations, tender requirements, and international reference pricing.

framework.title: Pricing Framework
framework.description: Your organization's pricing methodology: cost-plus, value-based, competitive parity, or hybrid. Define the principles that guide individual product pricing.

consistency.title: Cross-Product Consistency
consistency.description: Ensure related products are priced logically relative to each other. Avoid situations where a simpler product costs more than a complex one.

corridors.title: Geographic Price Corridors
corridors.description: Manage price variations across markets while preventing parallel imports and maintaining reference pricing compliance.

reimbursement.title: Reimbursement Alignment
reimbursement.description: Map pricing to reimbursement codes and rates in each market. Pricing above reimbursement limits adoption; pricing too far below leaves margin on the table.

pricingStrategy.tip: In markets with reference pricing (e.g., EU), your launch price in one country can cap your price in others. Plan your market entry sequence strategically.
